Behavioural
Design Patterns

Design Patterns Part 3
Lucky Nkosi · S10
9 Patterns · Quick Reference

Master mnemonic "Can Cats Irritate Many Meowing Observers Sometimes? Templates!"
Chain Command Iterator Mediator Memento Observer State Strategy Template
Nine patterns
01
Chain of Responsibility
no common alias
CoR
Intent Pass a request along a chain of handlers — each decides to handle or forward it
Roles
Handler BaseHandler ConcreteHandler Client
Key API setNext(h)   handle(req)
Use when Multiple objects may handle a request; right handler unknown to client; dynamic handler set
Real life Middleware pipelines, logging levels, auth filters, event bubbling
HBCC  Handler → BaseHandler → ConcreteHandler → Client
02
Command
also: Action, Transaction
CMD
Intent Encapsulate a request as a stand-alone object — enables undo, queuing, and logging
Roles
Command ConcreteCommand Receiver Client Invoker
Key API execute()   undo()
Use when Need undo/redo, job queues, deferred execution, operation logging, or transactions
Real life Ctrl+Z in editors, job queues, GUI buttons/shortcuts, macro recording
CRICQ  Command · Receiver · Invoker · Client · Queue/Undo
03
Iterator
also: Cursor
ITR
Intent Traverse any collection uniformly without exposing its internal representation
Roles
Iterator ConcreteIterator IterableCollection ConcreteCollection
Key API getNext()   hasMore()   createIterator()
Use when Polymorphic iteration over different collection types; multiple parallel traversals
Real life Java for-each loop, Python iterators, database cursors, social graph traversal
Cursor analogy  tracks position like a cursor in a document
04
Mediator
no common alias
MED
Intent Centralise all inter-object communication through one mediator — stops objects talking directly
Roles
Mediator ConcreteMediator Component ×n
Key API notify(sender)   reactOnX()
Use when Many objects communicate in complex ways; reusing objects is hard due to many references
Real life Chat rooms, UI dialog controllers, air traffic control, event buses
ATC  All Talk via Controller — planes talk to tower, never each other
05
Memento
also: Token, Snapshot
MEM
Intent Save & restore an object's state without violating its encapsulation
Roles
Originator Memento Caretaker
Key API save()→Memento   restore(m)
Use when Snapshot of state needed for undo; direct attribute access would break encapsulation
Real life Game saves, text editor undo, database transaction rollback
OCC  Originator · Caretaker · Can't-peek — Caretaker holds but never reads
06
Observer
also: Pub-Sub, Listener, Dependents
OBS
Intent Automatically notify a set of observers when a subject's state changes
Roles
Publisher Subscriber ConcretePublisher ConcreteSubscriber
Key API subscribe(s)   unsubscribe(s)   notify()   update(ctx)
Use when State change in one object must trigger unknown numbers of other objects
Real life DOM event listeners, React state, message queues, news feeds, stock tickers
YouTube analogy  subscribe once → auto-notified on every new upload
07
State
also: Objects for States
STA
Intent Replace conditional state logic with state objects that alter behaviour when swapped
Roles
Context State ConcreteState ×n
Key API changeState(state)   ConcreteState can self-transition
Use when Object behaviour depends on state; large multipart conditionals switch on state
Real life Order lifecycle, TCP connection states, vending machines, traffic lights
CSC  Context · State · ConcreteStates — states know the Context
08
Strategy
also: Policy
STR
Intent Encapsulate a family of algorithms and make them interchangeable at runtime
Roles
Context Strategy ConcreteStrategy ×n
Key API setStrategy(s)   execute(data)   Client swaps strategies
Use when Need to swap algorithms at runtime; a class defines many behaviours via conditionals
Real life Sort algorithms, routing (road/walk/transit), payment methods, compression
Google Maps  same Navigator — Road, Walk, Transit are swappable strategies
09
Template Method
no common alias
TMP
Intent Define an algorithm's skeleton in a superclass; subclasses override specific steps
Roles
AbstractClass ConcreteClass ×n
Key API templateMethod() (final) calls abstract primitive operations
Use when Algorithm structure never changes; only step implementations vary across subclasses
Real life Data miners (PDF/CSV/DOC), test frameworks, HTTP request processors
Recipe Rule  recipe fixed; each chef fills in their own ingredient details
Common confusion — key distinctions
State vs Strategy
State objects know the Context and can trigger their own transitions via context.changeState().
Strategy objects are independent — the client calls setStrategy() to swap.
Command vs Strategy
Command encapsulates a request (action + params + undo logic).
Strategy encapsulates an algorithm (how to do something). Commands often support history; strategies don't.
CoR vs Decorator
Both link objects in a chain. CoR can break the flow and routes messages.
Decorator always completes the chain and adds functionality.
Mediator vs Observer
Mediator is two-way — components call the mediator to talk to others.
Observer is one-way broadcast — publisher fires; subscribers receive.
Template Method vs Strategy
Template uses inheritance — subclass overrides steps; structure is fixed in superclass.
Strategy uses composition — inject an algorithm object; full replacement allowed.
Memento vs Command (for undo)
Memento captures a full state snapshot — restores everything at once.
Command captures the action and knows how to reverse it step by step.
OOP principles upheld
Single Responsibility
CoR Command Iterator Mediator State
Open / Closed
CoR Command Iterator Observer State Strategy
Encapsulation
Memento Iterator Command
Low Coupling
Mediator Observer CoR Strategy
Composition over Inheritance
Strategy Observer Mediator
Inheritance
Template State Iterator
